claim_text: The first video game is an inadequate question because electronic play, computer play, raster-TV apparatus and commercial industry have different thresholds.
contradiction_notes: Does not dismiss OXO, Tennis for Two or Spacewar! as non-games.

claim_text: The pioneer-patent argument concerns a television-type raster apparatus with player-controlled symbols, hitting, coincidence and response.
contradiction_notes: Earlier games remain historically important but technically distinct.

claim_text: Sinclair was central to UK home computing, but the BBC/Acorn educational route and Commodore route also shaped the industry.
The ZX Spectrum display used 6,912 bytes: 6,144 bitmap bytes and 768 colour-attribute bytes, creating colour-attribute constraints.
Tynesoft is associated with Blaydon and a move from educational software into games/publishing.
A retrospective account recalls a Washington Galleries computer club and later Oxclose Community Centre as meeting places for early programmers.
public_site_location: Before the Studios and Research Status

claim_text: Icon, Audiogenic/ASL and several North East authors are connected in surviving recollections, but the nature of any organisational transfer is unresolved.
contradiction_notes: Do not display as legal succession.

claim_text: Early Reflections should be presented through BBC/Electron work and Superior relationships before later Driver/Ubisoft history.
contradiction_notes: Early details need stronger contemporary sources.

claim_text: Tynesoft-to-Zeppelin is currently a staff/founder heritage lead, not a proven legal succession line.
contradiction_notes: Companies House supports later name chain, not every origin claim.

claim_text: Teesside should be researched as a separate regional orbit rather than collapsed into Newcastle/Tyneside.
contradiction_notes: Replace poster leads with stronger sources.
public_site_location: Teesside case study and regional map

claim_text: Modern North East examples such as Atomhawk, Ubisoft Reflections and People Can Fly Gateshead should be epilogue evidence, not proof of one uninterrupted lineage.
Newcastle's Game Technology Lab bridges regional skills, game-engineering teaching, industry relationships and the later university story.
contradiction_notes: Phase 1 handles the lab in detail.
